summary: End-to-end testing of RouteIQ DP Optimizer (Held-Karp). Backend: 9/9 pytest cases pass (root, health, optimize 2/5 nodes, min/max validation, haversine missing+full, history). Frontend: All key flows verified — topbar shows 'C++ SOLVER · READY', sidebars render, run-button disabled state correct, all 3 presets (DELHI-NCR/MANHATTAN/RANDOM) populate correctly, click-to-drop adds nodes, manual XY adds node and triggers error toast on empty input, RUN HELD-KARP renders animated tour with arrows + edge distance badges + DP telemetry + tour ledger + bottom status 'OPTIMAL Σ=...', remove-node button works, clear works.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: `logger` is referenced inside optimize() (line 189) but defined at module bottom (line 211). Works because module loads fully before requests, but stylistically should be at top.
server.py: `subproblems = n * states_explored` is computed in Python regardless of solver internals (it's a theoretical bound, not actual). Acceptable for telemetry display.
Manual X/Y inputs are type='number' so non-numeric chars cannot be typed — NaN scenario from spec is unreachable via UI; empty-input validation toast works correctly.
App.js is ~1180 lines — could be split into smaller modules (Sidebar, Canvas, ResultsPanel) for maintainability.
seed_data_creation: MongoDB optimization history populated organically by running optimize calls during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_routeiq_api.py (9 tests, all passing). C++ binary at /app/backend/route_optimizer is compiled and executable. All data-testids documented in review request match implementation. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env for both backend curls and frontend e2e.
